jd_text: On behalf of a prestigious high-end restaurant and lounge , we are recruiting a Head Hostess to join a luxury hospitality establishment. Location: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania Contract: Expatriation Role Overview The Head Hostess will be the first and last impression of the establishment, embodying elegance, authority, and discretion. She will oversee: VIP guest handling Reservation strategy & table positioning Client relationship continuity Hostess team leadership & training Front entrance coordination Representation of ownership in daily operations Key Responsibilities VIP Guest Management Personally welcome and seat VIP clients Maintain a confidential VIP database Manage table allocation based on hierarchy Handle special requests with discretion Ensure continuity for returning guests Oversee private dining experiences Face of Ownership Represent the owner’s standards and values Handle sensitive situations with diplomacy Maintain elegance and authority at all times Reservation & Guest Flow Management Oversee reservation systems Manage peak-hour capacity Balance exclusivity with revenue optimization Coordinate with Restaurant Manager, Executive Chef, and Lounge Manager Hostess Team Leadership Recruit and train hostesses Set grooming and behavior standards Lead daily briefings and performance reviews Ensure consistency and excellence across shifts Brand Experience Control first impressions (music, lighting, fragrance) Ensure guests feel recognized and valued Protect the exclusivity of the brand Candidate Profile Minimum 5+ years in luxury hospitality (5-star hotels, fine dining, private clubs) Experience as: Head Hostess Maitre d' Guest Relations Manager Experience with high-net-worth individuals, diplomats, executives Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus) Strong cultural awareness and international exposure Asian luxury hospitality training is a plus Personal Attributes Exceptional composure under pressure High emotional intelligence Discretion & confidentiality Elegant assertiveness Strong attention to detail Excellent memory for names and preferences Natural authority and cultural diplomacy
